headline: Geopolitical Shock Rips Through Equities as Iran Tensions Shatter Broad Market
body_html:
Wednesday's session turned decisively negative after President Trump declared that a memorandum of understanding with Iran was finished, jolting markets that had already been navigating a fragile geopolitical backdrop. The damage was widespread: of 395 names tracked, only 123 finished higher against 270 decliners, a lopsided ratio that underscored how little shelter buyers could find. The S&P 500 retreated in sympathy, and Britain's FTSE 100 suffered its worst single-session drop since May on the same catalyst.

Sector rotation told a stark story. Homebuilders shed 4.25% to lead the carnage, with Consumer names off 2.25%, Materials down 2.64%, Healthcare losing 1.71%, and Software retreating 1.34%. Even Industrials slipped 1.14%. The lone islands of green were Semiconductors, which added 2.4%, and Energy, up 1.22% — the latter buoyed by renewed concern over Strait of Hormuz shipping disruptions after war insurers began advising vessel operators to pause transits of the waterway.

Akamai was Wednesday's standout mover, surging 10.67% as internet-infrastructure names caught a bid alongside networking peer Arista Networks, up 8.75%. Together they illustrated how infrastructure spending themes can decouple from a broader risk-off tape. The IMF's concurrent cut to its 2026 global growth forecast — down to 3% — offered a sobering coda to an already bruising afternoon.

briefs.headline: Hormuz Shipping Halt Warnings Rattle Energy and Trade Corridors
briefs.body: War-risk insurers began counseling shipowners to suspend voyages through the Strait of Hormuz following a fresh round of attacks in the region. The advisory amplified Energy-sector outperformance on Wednesday, with the group gaining 1.22% even as the broader market sold off sharply. Analysts note that sustained disruption to the strait could meaningfully tighten global oil supply.

briefs.headline: Akamai Rockets More Than 10% to Top the Session's Leaderboard
briefs.body: Akamai surged 10.67% on Wednesday, the largest single-day gain among the names tracked, with Arista Networks close behind at 8.75%. The paired move lifted the Internet-Infrastructure and Networking subsectors against a deeply negative tape. Infrastructure-spending conviction appeared to override the session's prevailing risk-off sentiment for select technology names.

briefs.headline: Homebuilders Absorb the Sharpest Sector Blow, Off More Than 4%
briefs.body: The Homebuilders group fell 4.25%, the steepest sectoral decline on Wednesday, as rising geopolitical uncertainty and the IMF's lowered growth outlook weighed on rate-sensitive names. Consumer stocks were not far behind, shedding 2.25%. The dual pressure of macro headwinds and geopolitical stress left cyclical and housing-adjacent names with few defenders.

briefs.headline: IMF Trims 2026 Global Growth View to 3%, Eyes 2027 Recovery
briefs.body: The International Monetary Fund reduced its forecast for world economic expansion this year to 3%, citing ongoing trade frictions and geopolitical instability, while projecting a modest rebound in 2027. The downgrade arrived on an already turbulent session and added a fundamental dimension to what had initially appeared to be a sentiment-driven selloff.

briefs.headline: Ollie's Bargain Outlet Slides 9% to Headline the Decliner List
briefs.body: Ollie's Bargain Outlet dropped 9.04%, the steepest loss among individual names tracked Wednesday, reinforcing the broad weakness in Consumer-Retail. Capri Holdings fell 7.75% in the Consumer-Luxury segment, suggesting pressure was not confined to value-oriented retail but spread across the discretionary spectrum as risk appetite deteriorated.

briefs.headline: NATO Summit Claims Renewed Unity as Trade Spat With Spain Erupts
briefs.body: NATO Secretary-General Rutte declared the alliance reunited following an Ankara summit, describing earlier friction with Washington as resolved. Hours later, however, President Trump ordered a halt to U.S. trade with Spain over that country's NATO spending levels and its stance on Iran, injecting a fresh transatlantic complication into an already unsettled geopolitical week.
